title: triticum vulgare var. velutinum
intro: Triticum vulgare var. velutinum, commonly known as velvety or hairy wild rye, is a subspecies of the wheat grass (Triticum vulgare) and belongs to the Poaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Triticum vulgare var. velutinum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used topically in the form of salves or creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
